revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飛梅]]
**
{{multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | header = Flying Plum | header_align = center | footer = | background color = #FFF8DC | image1 = Tobiume02.jpg | width1 = 250 | caption1 = In mid‐February, the Flying Plum is in full bloom, heralding early spring. | image2 = Tobiume (summer).JPG | width2 = 250 | caption2 = In early September, the Flying Plum shows the colors of early autumn. }} '''Flying Plum'''' (Tobiume) is the name of the plum tree known as the sacred tree of {{ill|Dazaifu Tenmangū|en|Dazaifu Tenmangū|qid=Q710786|lt=Dazaifu Tenmangu|1=Dazaifu Tenmangū}}, located in {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|qid=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu City|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}} in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It originally stood in the precincts of {{ill|Enkasha|ja|榎社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkasha|1=Enoki-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541869}}, which was established on the site of the former residence ({{ill|Southern Annex of the Government Office|ja|府の南館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Annex of the Government Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} in the old {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}}, {{ill|Mikasa District|ja|御笠郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa District|1=Mikasa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071211}} {{ill|Dazaifu Village|ja|太宰府村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Village|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太宰府市|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q728218}}, but when Dazaifu Tenmangu was constructed, the tree was transplanted to a spot in front of the main hall. This white plum, said to be over 1,000 years old, is planted to the left of the main hall (i.e. on the right when facing it) and consists of three separate plants.
== Overview == It is recognized as the first among the plums planted at Dazaifu Tenmangu to begin blooming.
== Legend of the Flying Plum == According to legend, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the noble {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, after losing a political struggle against {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|qid=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara Tokihira|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} within the imperial court of {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, was exiled far away to Dazaifu in the first year of the {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi era|1=Engi (era)}} (901). As he bid farewell to the trees of his estate—especially the plum, cherry, and pine trees he dearly loved—he is said to have recited the following poem as if speaking to the plum tree: {{quote| “Should the east wind blow, May it spread fragrance— O plum blossoms, Though your master is gone, Do not forget the coming of spring.” ― First recorded in the {{ill|Shūi Wakashū|en|Shūi Wakashū|qid=Q1277569|lt=Shūi Wakashū|1=Shūi Wakashū}}, Volume 16, Zasshun. }}
The legend narrates that among the garden trees that adored Michizane, the cherry tree, upon learning that its master had departed for a faraway land, lost its leaves in sorrow and eventually withered. In contrast, the plum and pine trees, eager to follow him, took flight. However, the pine exhausted its strength along the way and descended near Itabusa in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|Itabusa, Mikasa District|ja|八部郡板宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabusa, Mikasa District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day Itabusa, near {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Suma-ku, Kobe|en|Suma-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1155010|lt=Suma Ward|1=Suma-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Itabusa Town|ja|板宿町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabusa Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}), where it took root—a tale known as the {{Anchors|Tobimatsu legend|飛松伝説}}. Meanwhile, the lone plum is said to have miraculously flown all the way to Dazaifu in a single night, landing there.
A hint of this legend is found in the ''{{ill|Kitano_Tenjin_Engi_Emaki|en|Kitano_Tenjin_Engi_Emaki|qid=Q3197562|lt=Kitano Tenjin Engi Emaki|1=Kitano Tenjin Engi Emaki}}'', a national treasure. In its accompanying text, a line is added at the end of the section featuring the “east wind” poem that reads, “Thus, it is said that the plum has flown to Tsukushi,” suggesting the origin of the Flying Plum legend. Originally, the Tenjin faith—once regarding Michizane as a vengeful spirit or curse—later evolved (especially during the tumultuous {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}) when a monk from {{ill|Tōfuku-ji|en|Tōfuku-ji|qid=Q2166743|lt=Tōfuku-ji|1=Tōfuku-ji}} reportedly dreamt of a transformation, causing his once fierce visage to soften. From the Muromachi period onward, Zen monks began to emphasize the admiration of plum blossoms.
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:Dazaifu Tenmangu Plum Tree 2004-03-07.jpg|During the plum blossom season, the area teems with visitors.<ref>Note: Traditionally, plums are best appreciated at the onset of bloom rather than at full bloom, unlike cherry blossoms.</ref> |File:Tobiume Notice Board 20050220.jpg|A notice board explaining the Flying Plum }}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōbai-den|ja|紅梅殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbai-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at Dazaifu Tenmangu) * {{ill|Tobimatsu|ja|飛松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinboku|en|Shinboku|qid=Q48743901|lt=Sacred Tree|1=Shinboku}} * {{ill|Umegae mochi|en|Umegae mochi|qid=Q11538378|lt=Plum Cake on a Plum Branch|1=Umegae mochi}} * {{ill|Bush Warbler Replacement|ja|鷽替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush Warbler Replacement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11676444|1=Bush Warbler Replacement}} * {{ill|Grand Kitano Tea Ceremony|ja|北野大茶会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Great Tea Gathering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北野大茶湯|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404273|1=Grand Kitano Tea Ceremony}} * {{ill|Japanese spirit, Chinese learning|ja|和魂漢才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit, Chinese Talent|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418249|1=Japanese spirit, Chinese learning}} * {{ill|Prunus mume|ja|梅鉢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plum Bowl|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q157763|1=Prunus mume}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamadata Tateki|ja|大和田建樹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamadata Tateki|1=Takeki Ōwada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11261562}}: Referenced in song no. 41 of the second collection of Railroad Songs (Sanyō–Kyūshū Edition). * {{ill|Masashi Sada|en|Masashi Sada|qid=Q1059718|lt=Sadamasashi|1=Masashi Sada}}: His 1977 album {{ill|Weather vane|en|Weather vane|qid=Q524738|lt=Wind Vane|1=Weather vane}} includes a track titled “Flying Plum” set in Dazaifu. * The 45th National Sports Festival, colloquially known as the “Tobiume National Games,” held in Fukuoka Prefecture in 1990. * The “Tobiume” school trip train, an affectionate nickname for a train service between Kyushu and Kansai. * The “Tobiume Credit Union,” a financial institution primarily operating in Fukuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Naruka Hime/Mikoto|ja|鳴花ヒメ・ミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruka Hime/Mikoto|1=MEIKA Hime · Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q63213697}}: A VOCALOID released on March 30, 2019, by Gainoid Inc., inspired by the Flying Plum.
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{translated page|ja|飛梅|version=98825296|comment=Imported full ja history}}
